title: Figure 15 — Stored Energy Operating Mechanism
source: Siemens 38kV Vacuum Circuit Breaker Type 38-3AF, Instruction Manual SG-3528, p.12 (Interrupter/Operator Description)
note: Callout numbers match the manual's part legend. Descriptions are drawn from the manual's Interrupter/Operator Description (pp. 12-14).

hotspots.name: Manual spring charging access hole
hotspots.desc: Opening into which the manual charging crank (50) is inserted to hand-charge the closing spring when control power is unavailable.

hotspots.name: Charging mechanism gear box
hotspots.desc: Gear box in the mechanism housing that transmits torque from the charging motor (50.4) or hand crank to the charging shaft. Requires no maintenance.

hotspots.name: Flange
hotspots.desc: Flange on the charging drive. As the hand crank or motor turns, the flange rotates until the driver (50.3.1) locates in the cutaway part of the cam disc (62.3), coupling the drive to the charging shaft.

hotspots.name: Driver
hotspots.desc: Engages the cutaway in the cam disc (62.3) so the charging shaft follows the charging drive and charges the closing spring.

hotspots.name: Drive lever
hotspots.desc: As the closing spring (62) discharges, the cam disc (62.3) actuates the drive lever, which drives the jack shaft (63) via the trip-free coupling rod (62.8) to close the breaker.

hotspots.name: Cam disc
hotspots.desc: Cam on the charging shaft. The driver (50.3.1) locates in its cutaway to drive charging; it then follows idly while the spring is charged, coming into position for closing.

hotspots.name: Latching pawl
hotspots.desc: Securely locks lever (62.5) — and therefore the fully charged closing spring — until a close command releases it.

hotspots.name: Lever
hotspots.desc: Lever at the end of the charging shaft; held by the latching pawl (62.5.2) once the closing spring is charged.

hotspots.name: Close pushbutton
hotspots.desc: Local CLOSE command. Pressing it releases the charged closing spring (62) to close the breaker. Remote closing uses the spring release coil 52SRC (53.1) instead.

hotspots.name: Callout not defined in the legend
hotspots.desc: This callout number does not appear in the Figure 15 part legend. It points to the wiring/terminal area near the auxiliary switch (68). Treat as unverified rather than assuming a part name.

hotspots.name: Control lever
hotspots.desc: Actuated by the crank (62.2); moves the linkage (55.1) to update the close-open and spring-charge indicators, and cancels the 'spring charged' indication as the spring discharges.

hotspots.name: Crank
hotspots.desc: Crank at one end of the charging shaft. When the closing spring is fully charged, it drives the control lever (55.2) / linkage (55.1) to give the 'closing spring charged' indication (55).

hotspots.name: Closing spring
hotspots.desc: The stored-energy spring. When charged (by motor 50.4 or hand crank 50) and released, it supplies the mechanical energy to close the breaker. Automatically recharged after each close for auto-reclose duty.

hotspots.name: Linkage
hotspots.desc: Links the crank (62.2) / control lever (55.2) to the indicators, transmitting mechanism state to the close-open indicator (55) and spring-charge indicator (58).

hotspots.name: Spring charging motor
hotspots.desc: Motor bolted to the gear box (50.2) that automatically charges the closing spring, recharging it after a close so the mechanism is ready for the next operation. Requires no maintenance.

hotspots.name: Closing spring charge indicator
hotspots.desc: Shows whether the closing spring is CHARGED or DISCHARGED.

hotspots.name: Limit switches
hotspots.desc: Switch the motor control supply: they cut motor supply when the closing spring is fully charged, and restore it after a close so the spring recharges immediately.

hotspots.name: Close-open indicator
hotspots.desc: Indicates breaker main-contact state (OPEN / CLOSED), driven from the jack-shaft linkage via control lever (55.2) and linkage (55.1).

hotspots.name: Operation counter
hotspots.desc: Mechanical counter that increments with each breaker operation.

hotspots.name: Auxiliary switch
hotspots.desc: Multi-contact switch actuated by the jack shaft (63) through lever (63.1) and link (68.1). Provides breaker-status contacts for control and indication circuits.

hotspots.name: Spring release coil 52SRC
hotspots.desc: On a remote CLOSE command, this coil unlatches the closing spring — the electrical equivalent of pressing the close pushbutton (53).

hotspots.name: Shunt trip coil 52T
hotspots.desc: Converts an electrical tripping pulse into mechanical energy that releases the trip latch and opens the breaker on remote or protective-relay command.

hotspots.name: Link
hotspots.desc: Connects the jack-shaft lever (63.1) to the auxiliary switch (68), so switch contacts follow the breaker position.

hotspots.name: Trip pushbutton
hotspots.desc: Local TRIP command. Pressing it releases the tripping spring (64) to open the breaker. Remote/protective tripping uses the shunt trip coil 52T (54.1).

hotspots.name: Pawl
hotspots.desc: Part of the closed-position latch that, with pawl (64.2) and pawl roller (64.3.1), holds the breaker mechanically latched closed.

hotspots.name: Spring return latch
hotspots.desc: Carried inside the trip-free coupling rod (62.8) to keep it rigid. When the breaker is not in CONNECT/TEST or when a trip is applied, it lets the rod go slack — decoupling the drive lever from the jack shaft (trip-free action).

hotspots.name: Pawl
hotspots.desc: Latches the breaker in the closed position against the pawl roller (64.3.1).

hotspots.name: Lever
hotspots.desc: Lever in the closed-position trip-latch assembly.

hotspots.name: Callout not defined in the legend
hotspots.desc: This callout number does not appear in the Figure 15 part legend. It points to the mechanism base / frame at the bottom left. Treat as unverified rather than assuming a part name.

hotspots.name: Shock absorber
hotspots.desc: Hydraulic shock absorber that dampens the operating mechanism at the end of its travel.

hotspots.name: Tripping spring
hotspots.desc: Charged during closing (via jack-shaft lever 63.5). When released by the trip button (54) or shunt trip coil (54.1) it turns the jack shaft (63) through lever (63.5) to open the breaker.

hotspots.name: Trip free coupling rod
hotspots.desc: Forms the link between the drive lever (62.6) and the jack shaft (63). Its rigidity depends on the spring return latch (62.8.1); this lets a trip command override a close at any time ('trip free').

hotspots.name: Trip free coupling link
hotspots.desc: With the coupling lever (62.8.3), makes the spring-return-latch position depend on the racking interlock, so the drive decouples whenever the breaker is not in CONNECT/TEST.

hotspots.name: Jack shaft
hotspots.desc: Main output shaft. Rotated by the closing drive to close and by the tripping spring (64) via lever (63.5) to open. Operates the three insulated pole couplers and, through link (68.1), the auxiliary switch (68).

hotspots.name: Lever
hotspots.desc: Lever on the jack shaft. The tripping spring (64) acts through it to open the breaker; it also charges the tripping spring during closing.

hotspots.name: Trip free coupling lever
hotspots.desc: With coupling link (62.8.2), ties the spring-return-latch (62.8.1) position to the racking interlock so a trip decouples the drive lever from the jack shaft. (Located behind 62.8.)
